other_observers: John Shenot, David Wade, Austin Hess, Roger and Brenda Linfield, Gary Matthews, John Vanderpoel, Bill Kaempfer, Kathleen Sullivan
description: Bird was sitting on shore line, foraging along shore, and flying along shore. Intermediate in size between Franklin's Gulls and Ring-billed Gulls in its company. Elongated body like Ring-billed Gull. Dark gray upperparts with black wingtips. Whitish underparts darkening to pale gray on upper breast. Head pale, mostly whitish with gray smudge on nape and sides of the head. Black eye and bill. Flat-headed appearance. Dark gray legs and feet. Bill heavier and longer than that of Franklin's Gulls, and slightly drooped. Back feathers paler gray than definitive adult. In flight and when sitting, primaries totally black lacking white tips. Secondaries and inner primaries tipped white, forming a white trailing edge to the wing in flight. Outermost primary, p10, unmolted and worn on both wings. p9 apparently missing (dropped off) from both wings. Apparently almost fully molted into 2nd basic plumage. Tail was entirely white. No vocalizations.
photographer: Photographer: N. Komar, N. Komar, N. Komar, N. Komar, N. Komar
